name: python-liquid2
documentation:
Python Liquid2

Liquid templates for Python, with some extra features.

--- **Table of Contents** - [Install](#install) - [Links](#links) - [Quick start](#quick-start) - [Related Projects](#related-projects) ## Install Install Python Liquid2 from [PyPi](https://pypi.org/project/python-liquid2/) using [pip](https://pip.pypa.io/en/stable/getting-started/): ```console python -m pip install python-liquid2 ``` Or [Pipenv](https://pipenv.pypa.io/en/latest/): ```console pipenv install python-liquid2 ``` Or [Poetry](https://python-poetry.org/): ```console poetry add python-liquid2 ``` ## Links - Documentation: TODO - Change Log: https://github.com/jg-rp/python-liquid2/blob/main/CHANGES.md - PyPi: TODO - Source Code: https://github.com/jg-rp/python-liquid2 - Issue Tracker: https://github.com/jg-rp/python-liquid2/issues ## Quick start ### `render()` Here's a very simple example that renders a template from a string of text with the package-level `render()` function. The template has just one placeholder variable `you`, which we've given the value `"World"`. ```python from liquid2 import render print(render("Hello, {{ you }}!", you="World")) # Hello, World! ``` ### `parse()` Often you'll want to render the same template several times with different variables. We can parse source text without immediately rendering it using the `parse()` function. `parse()` returns a `Template` instance with a `render()` method. ```python from liquid2 import parse template = parse("Hello, {{ you }}!") print(template.render(you="World")) # Hello, World! print(template.render(you="Liquid")) # Hello, Liquid! ``` ### Configure Both `parse()` and `render()` are convenience functions that use the default Liquid environment. For all but the simplest cases you'll want to configure an instance of `Environment`, then load and render templates from that. ```python from liquid2 import CachingFileSystemLoader from liquid2 import Environment env = Environment( auto_escape=True, loader=CachingFileSystemLoader("./templates"), ) ``` Then, using `env.from_string()` or `env.get_template()`, we can create a `Template` from a string or read from the file system, respectively. ```python # ... continued from above template = env.from_string("Hello, {{ you }}!") print(template.render(you="World")) # Hello, World! # Try to load "./templates/index.html" another_template = env.get_template("index.html") data = {"some": {"thing": [1, 2, 3]}} result = another_template.render(**data) ``` Unless you happen to have a relative folder called `templates` with a file called `index.html` within it, we would expect a `TemplateNotFoundError` to be raised when running the example above.

modules.name: liquid2.context
documentation: Template render context.

classes.name: liquid2.context.RenderContext

methods.name: liquid2.context.RenderContext.assign
documentation: Add _key_ to the local namespace with value _val_.

methods.name: liquid2.context.RenderContext.get
documentation: Resolve the variable _path_ in the current namespace.

methods.name: liquid2.context.RenderContext.get_async
documentation: Asynchronously resolve the variable _path_ in the current namespace.

methods.name: liquid2.context.RenderContext.resolve
documentation: Resolve variable _name_ in the current scope.

methods.name: liquid2.context.RenderContext.get_item
documentation:
An item getter used when resolving a Liquid path.

Override this to change the behavior of `.first`, `.last` and `.size`.

methods.name: liquid2.context.RenderContext.get_item_async
documentation: An async item getter for resolving paths.

methods.name: liquid2.context.RenderContext.filter
documentation: Return the filter callable for _name_.

methods.name: liquid2.context.RenderContext.get_size_of_locals
documentation:
Return the "size" or a "score" for the current local namespace.

This is used by the optional local namespace resource limit. Override
`get_size_of_locals` to customize how the limit is calculated. Be sure
to consider `self.local_namespace_size_carry` when writing a custom
implementation of `get_size_of_locals`.

The default implementation uses `sys.getsizeof()` on each of the local
namespace's values. It is not a reliable measure of size in bytes.

methods.name: liquid2.context.RenderContext.extend
documentation: Extend this context with the given read-only namespace.

methods.name: liquid2.context.RenderContext.copy
documentation: Return a copy of this render context with a new scope.

methods.name: liquid2.context.RenderContext.stopindex
documentation: Set or return the stop index of a for loop.

methods.name: liquid2.context.RenderContext.loop
documentation: Just like `Context.extend`, but keeps track of ForLoop objects too.

methods.name: liquid2.context.RenderContext.parentloop
documentation: Return the last ForLoop object from the loop stack.

methods.name: liquid2.context.RenderContext.raise_for_loop_limit
documentation: Raise a `LoopIterationLimitError` if loop stack is bigger than the limit.

methods.name: liquid2.context.RenderContext.get_output_buffer
documentation: Return a new output buffer respecting any limits set on the environment.

methods.name: liquid2.context.RenderContext.markup
documentation: Return a _safe_ string if auto escape is enabled.

methods.name: liquid2.context.RenderContext.cycle
documentation: Return the index of the next item in the named cycle.

methods.name: liquid2.context.RenderContext.increment
documentation: Increment the named counter and return its value.

methods.name: liquid2.context.RenderContext.decrement
documentation: Decrement the named counter and return its value.
Template render state.

classes.name: liquid2.context.BuiltIn
documentation: Mapping-like object for resolving built-in, dynamic objects.

modules.name: liquid2.undefined
documentation:
Manage undefined template variables.

When rendering a Liquid template, if a variable name can not be resolved, an instance of
liquid.Undefined, or a subclass, is used instead.

functions.name: liquid2.undefined.is_undefined
documentation: Return `True` if `obj` is undefined. `False` otherwise.

classes.name: liquid2.undefined.Undefined

methods.name: liquid2.undefined.Undefined.poke
documentation: Prod the type, giving it the opertunity to raise an exception.

The default undefined type.

Always evaluates to an empty string. Can be iterated over and indexed without error.

classes.name: liquid2.undefined.DebugUndefined
documentation: An undefined that returns debug information when rendered.

classes.name: liquid2.undefined.StrictUndefined
documentation: An undefined that raises an exception for everything other than `repr`.

modules.name: liquid2.filter
documentation: Filter function helpers.

functions.name: liquid2.filter.bool_arg
documentation: Return _True_ if _value_ is liquid truthy.

functions.name: liquid2.filter.mapping_arg
documentation: Make sure _value_ is a mapping type.

functions.name: liquid2.filter.int_arg
documentation: Return `val` as an int or `default` if `val` can't be cast to an int.

functions.name: liquid2.filter.num_arg
documentation:
Return `val` as an int or float.

If `val` can't be cast to an int or float, return `default`.

functions.name: liquid2.filter.decimal_arg
documentation:
Return _val_ as an int or decimal.

If _val_ can't be cast to an int or decimal, return `default`.

functions.name: liquid2.filter.with_context
documentation:
Pass the active render context to decorated filter functions.

If a function is decorated with `with_context`, that function should
accept a `context` keyword argument, being the active render context.

Args:
    _filter: The filter function to decorate.

functions.name: liquid2.filter.with_environment
documentation:
Pass the active environment to decorated filter functions.

If a function is decorated with `with_environment`, that function should
accept an `environment` keyword argument, being the active environment.

Args:
    _filter: The filter function to decorate.

functions.name: liquid2.filter.string_filter
documentation: A filter function decorator that converts the first argument to a string.

functions.name: liquid2.filter.sequence_filter
documentation:
Coerce the left value to sequence.

This is intended to mimic the semantics of the reference implementation's
`InputIterator` class.

functions.name: liquid2.filter.math_filter
documentation: Raise a `FilterArgumentError` if the filter value can not be a number.

functions.name: liquid2.filter._flatten
documentation: Flatten nested "liquid arrays" into a list.

modules.name: liquid2.output
documentation: Template output buffers.

classes.name: liquid2.output.LimitedStringIO
documentation: A StringIO subclass that limits the number of bytes that can be written.

classes.name: liquid2.output.NullIO

methods.name: liquid2.output.NullIO.write
documentation: Pretend to write the string to the stream.
A StringIO subclass that is a null op. It doesn't write anything.

modules.name: liquid2.tag
documentation: Base class for all Liquid tags.

classes.name: liquid2.tag.Tag

methods.name: liquid2.tag.Tag.parse
documentation: Return a parse tree node by parsing tokens from the given stream.
Base class for all built-in and custom template tags.

modules.name: liquid2.exceptions
documentation: Liquid specific Exceptions and warnings.

classes.name: liquid2.exceptions.LiquidError

methods.name: liquid2.exceptions.LiquidError.detailed_message
documentation: Return an error message formatted with extra context info.

methods.name: liquid2.exceptions.LiquidError.message
documentation: The exception's error message if one was given.

methods.name: liquid2.exceptions.LiquidError.context

returns.name: tuple[int, int, str, str, str] | None

documentation:
Return context information for this error.

Returns (line, col, previous line, current line, next line) or None
if no context information is available.
Base class for all Liquid exceptions.

classes.name: liquid2.exceptions.LiquidInterrupt
documentation: Loop interrupt exception.

classes.name: liquid2.exceptions.StopRender
documentation:
Template inheritance interrupt.

An interrupt used to signal that `BoundTemplate.render_with_context` should stop
rendering more nodes. This is used by template inheritance tags and is not an error
condition.

classes.name: liquid2.exceptions.LiquidEnvironmentError
documentation: An exception raised due to a misconfigured environment.

classes.name: liquid2.exceptions.LiquidSyntaxError
documentation: Exception raised when there is a parser error.

classes.name: liquid2.exceptions.TemplateInheritanceError
documentation:
An exceptions raised when template inheritance tags are used incorrectly.

This could occur when parsing a template or at render time.

classes.name: liquid2.exceptions.RequiredBlockError
documentation: An exception raised when a required block has not been overridden.

classes.name: liquid2.exceptions.LiquidTypeError
documentation: Exception raised when an error occurs at render time.

classes.name: liquid2.exceptions.LiquidIndexError
documentation: An exception raised when a sequence index is out of range.

classes.name: liquid2.exceptions.LiquidNameError
documentation: An exception raised when an unknown function extension is called.

classes.name: liquid2.exceptions.DisabledTagError
documentation: Exception raised when an attempt is made to render a disabled tag.

classes.name: liquid2.exceptions.NoSuchFilterFunc
documentation: Exception raised when a filter lookup fails.

classes.name: liquid2.exceptions.TemplateNotFoundError
documentation: Exception raised when a template could not be found.

classes.name: liquid2.exceptions.ResourceLimitError
documentation: Base class for exceptions relating to resource limits.

classes.name: liquid2.exceptions.ContextDepthError
documentation:
Exception raised when the maximum context depth is reached.

Usually indicates recursive use of `render` or `include` tags.

classes.name: liquid2.exceptions.LoopIterationLimitError
documentation: Exception raised when the loop iteration limit has been exceeded.

classes.name: liquid2.exceptions.OutputStreamLimitError
documentation: Exception raised when an output stream limit has been exceeded.

classes.name: liquid2.exceptions.LocalNamespaceLimitError
documentation: Exception raised when a local namespace limit has been exceeded.

classes.name: liquid2.exceptions.LiquidValueError
documentation: Exception raised when a cast from str to int exceeds the length limit.

classes.name: liquid2.exceptions.UndefinedError
documentation: Exception raised by the StrictUndefined type.

classes.name: liquid2.exceptions.BreakLoop
documentation: Exception raised when a BreakNode is rendered.

classes.name: liquid2.exceptions.ContinueLoop
documentation: Exception raised when a ContinueNode is rendered.

classes.name: liquid2.exceptions.CacheCapacityValueError
documentation: An exception raised when the LRU cache is given a zero or negative capacity.

modules.name: liquid2.limits
documentation: System wide limits.

functions.name: liquid2.limits.to_int
documentation:
Prevent DoS by very large str to int conversion.

See https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/95778

functions.name: liquid2.parse
documentation:
Parse _source_ as a Liquid template using the default environment.

Args:
    source: Liquid template source code.
    globals: Variables that will be available to the resulting template.

Return:
    A new template bound to the default environment.

functions.name: liquid2.render
documentation:
Parse and render _source_ as a Liquid template using the default environment.

Additional arguments are passed to `dict()` and will be available as template
variables.

Args:
    source: Liquid template source code.
    *args: dict-like arguments added to the template render context.
    **kwargs: dict-like arguments added to the template render context.

Return:
    The result of rendering _source_ as a Liquid template.

functions.name: liquid2.render_async
documentation:
Parse and render _source_ as a Liquid template using the default environment.

Additional arguments are passed to `dict()` and will be available as template
variables.

Args:
    source: Liquid template source code.
    *args: dict-like arguments added to the template render context.
    **kwargs: dict-like arguments added to the template render context.

Return:
    The result of rendering _source_ as a Liquid template.

Render the node to the output buffer.

methods.name: liquid2.ast.Node.template_scope

methods.name: liquid2.ast.Node.block_scope

methods.name: liquid2.ast.Node.partial_scope

Base class for all template nodes.

classes.name: liquid2.ast.PartialScope
documentation: The kind of scope a partial template should have when loaded.

class_variables.name: liquid2.ast.Partial.name

class_variables.name: liquid2.ast.Partial.scope
documentation: The kind of scope the partial template should have when loaded.

class_variables.name: liquid2.ast.Partial.in_scope
documentation: Names that will be added to the partial template scope.
Partial template meta data.

documentation: Render the node to the output buffer.

documentation: Render the node to the output buffer.

methods.name: liquid2.ast.BlockNode.children
documentation: Return this node's children.
A node containing a sequence of other nodes.

classes.name: liquid2.ast.ConditionalBlockNode

documentation: Render the node to the output buffer.

methods.name: liquid2.ast.ConditionalBlockNode.render_to_output_async
documentation: Render the node to the output buffer.

methods.name: liquid2.ast.ConditionalBlockNode.children
documentation: Return this node's children.

methods.name: liquid2.ast.ConditionalBlockNode.expressions
documentation: Return this node's expressions.

modules.name: liquid2.shopify
documentation: noqa: D104

modules.name: liquid2.loader
documentation: Base class for all template loaders.

classes.name: liquid2.loader.BaseLoader

methods.name: liquid2.loader.BaseLoader.get_source
documentation:
Get source information for a template.

Args:
    env: The `Environment` attempting to load the template source text.
    template_name: A name or identifier for a template's source text.
    context: An optional render context that can be used to narrow the template
        source search space.
    kwargs: Arbitrary arguments that can be used to narrow the template source
        search space.

methods.name: liquid2.loader.BaseLoader.get_source_async
documentation:
An async version of `get_source`.

The default implementation delegates to `get_source()`.

methods.name: liquid2.loader.BaseLoader.load
documentation:
Find and parse template source code.

Args:
    env: The `Environment` attempting to load the template source text.
    name: A name or identifier for a template's source text.
    globals: A mapping of render context variables attached to the
        resulting template.
    context: An optional render context that can be used to narrow the template
        source search space.
    kwargs: Arbitrary arguments that can be used to narrow the template source
        search space.

methods.name: liquid2.loader.BaseLoader.load_async
documentation: An async version of `load()`.
Base class for all template loaders.

classes.name: liquid2.loader.TemplateSource
documentation:
A Liquid template source as returned by the `get_source` method of a `loader`.

Attributes:
    source: The liquid template source code.
    name: The liquid template file name or other string identifying its origin.
    uptodate: Optional callable that will return `True` if the template is up to
        date, or `False` if it needs to be reloaded.
    matter: Optional mapping containing variables associated with the template.
        Could be "front matter" or other meta data.

modules.name: liquid2.shopify.tags
documentation: noqa: D104

modules.name: liquid2.stream
documentation: Step through a stream of tokens.

classes.name: liquid2.stream.TokenStream

methods.name: liquid2.stream.TokenStream.current
documentation: Return the item at self[0] without advancing the iterator.

methods.name: liquid2.stream.TokenStream.next
documentation: Return the next token and advance the iterator.

methods.name: liquid2.stream.TokenStream.peek
documentation: Return the item at self[1] without advancing the iterator.

methods.name: liquid2.stream.TokenStream.expect
documentation: Raise a _LiquidSyntaxError_ if the current token type doesn't match _typ_.

methods.name: liquid2.stream.TokenStream.expect_one_of
documentation: Raise a _LiquidSyntaxError_ if the current token type is not in _types_.

methods.name: liquid2.stream.TokenStream.expect_peek
documentation: Raise a _LiquidSyntaxError_ if the next token type does not match _typ_.

methods.name: liquid2.stream.TokenStream.expect_tag
documentation: Raise a syntax error if the current token is not a tag with _tag_name_.

methods.name: liquid2.stream.TokenStream.expect_eos
documentation: Raise a syntax error if we're not at the end of the stream.

methods.name: liquid2.stream.TokenStream.is_tag
documentation: Return _True_ if the current token is a tag named _tag_name_.

methods.name: liquid2.stream.TokenStream.is_word
documentation: Return _True_ if the current token is a word with value equal to _value_.

methods.name: liquid2.stream.TokenStream.peek_word
documentation: Return _True_ if the next token is a word with value equal to _value_.

methods.name: liquid2.stream.TokenStream.is_one_of
documentation: Return _True_ if the current token is a tag with a name in _tag_names_.

methods.name: liquid2.stream.TokenStream.peek_one_of
documentation: Return _True_ if the next token is a tag with a name in _tag_names_.

methods.name: liquid2.stream.TokenStream.into_inner
documentation:
Return a new stream over the current token's expression, consuming the token.

Raises:
    LiquidSyntaxError: if the current token is not a tag
Step through a stream of tokens.

modules.name: liquid2.template
documentation: A parsed template, ready to be rendered.

classes.name: liquid2.template.Template

methods.name: liquid2.template.Template.full_name
documentation: Return this template's path, if available, joined with its name.

methods.name: liquid2.template.Template.render
documentation: Render this template with _args_ and _kwargs_.

methods.name: liquid2.template.Template.render_async
documentation: Render this template with _args_ and _kwargs_.

methods.name: liquid2.template.Template.render_with_context
documentation: Render this template using an existing render context and output buffer.

methods.name: liquid2.template.Template.render_with_context_async
documentation: Render this template using an existing render context and output buffer.

methods.name: liquid2.template.Template.make_globals
documentation: Return a mapping including render arguments and template globals.

methods.name: liquid2.template.Template.analyze
documentation:
Statically analyze this template and any included/rendered templates.

Args:
    include_partials: If `True`, we will try to load partial templates and
        analyze those templates too.

methods.name: liquid2.template.Template.analyze_async
documentation: An async version of `analyze`.

methods.name: liquid2.template.Template.is_up_to_date
documentation: Return _False_ if the template has been modified, _True_ otherwise.

methods.name: liquid2.template.Template.is_up_to_date_async
documentation:
An async version of _is_up_to_date()_.

If _template.uptodate_ is a coroutine, it wil be awaited. Otherwise it will be
called just like _is_up_to_date_.
A parsed template ready to be rendered.

modules.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string
documentation: Filter functions that operate on strings.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.append
documentation:
Return a copy of _val_ concatenated with _arg_.

If _arg_ is not a string, it will be converted to one before concatenation.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.capitalize
documentation: Return _val_ with the first character in uppercase and the rest lowercase.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.downcase
documentation: Return a copy of _val_ with all characters converted to lowercase.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.escape
documentation: Return _val_ with the characters &, < and > converted to HTML-safe sequences.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.escape_once
documentation:
Return _val_ with the characters &, < and > converted to HTML-safe sequences.

It is safe to use `escape_one` on string values that already contain HTML escape
sequences.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.lstrip
documentation: Return a copy of _val_ with leading whitespace removed.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.newline_to_br
documentation:
Return a copy of _val_ with LF or CRLF converted to `
`, plus a newline.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.prepend
documentation: Return a copy of _arg_ concatenated with _val_.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.remove
documentation: Return a copy of _val_ with all occurrences of _arg_ removed.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.remove_first
documentation: Return a copy of _val_ with the first occurrence of _arg_ removed.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.remove_last
documentation: Return a copy of _val_ with last occurrence of _arg_ removed.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.replace
documentation: Return a copy of _val_ with each occurrence of _seq_ replaced with _sub_.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.replace_first
documentation: Return a copy of _val_ with the first occurrence of _seq_ replaced with _sub_.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.replace_last
documentation: Return a copy of _val_ with the last occurrence of _seq_ replaced with _sub_.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.upcase
documentation: Return a copy of _val_ with all characters converted to uppercase.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.slice_
documentation:
Return the subsequence of _val_ starting at _start_ with up to _length_ chars.

Array-like objects return a list, strings return a substring, all other objects are
cast to a string before returning a substring.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.split
documentation:
Split string _val_ on delimiter _sep_.

If _sep_ is empty or _undefined_, _val_ is split into a list of single
characters. If _val_ is empty or equal to _sep_, an empty list is returned.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.strip
documentation: Return a copy of _val_ with leading and trailing whitespace removed.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.rstrip
documentation: Return a copy of _val_ with trailing whitespace removed.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.strip_html
documentation: Return a copy of _val_ with all HTML tags removed.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.strip_newlines
documentation: Return ta copy of _val_ with all newline characters removed.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.truncate
documentation: Return a copy of _val_ truncated to _num_ characters.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.truncatewords
documentation: Return a copy of _val_ truncated to at most _num_ words.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.url_encode
documentation: Return a percent-encoded copy of _val_ so it is useable in a URL.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.url_decode
documentation: Return a copy of _val_ after decoding percent-encoded sequences.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.string.safe
documentation: Return a copy of _val_ that will not be automatically HTML escaped on output.

modules.name: liquid2.builtin.filters
documentation: noqa: D104

modules.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.misc
documentation: Miscellaneous filter functions.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.misc.size
documentation:
Return the length of _obj_.

_obj_ could be a dict, list, string or any class implementing _len_.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.misc.default
documentation: Return _obj_, or _default_ if _obj_ is nil, false, or empty.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.misc.date
documentation: Return a string representation of _dat_ using format string _fmt_.

modules.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.array
documentation: Filter functions that operate on arrays.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.array._getitem
documentation:
Helper for the map filter.

Same as sequence[key], but returns a default value if key does not exist
in sequence.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.array._lower
documentation: Helper for the sort filter.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.array.join
documentation: Return a string by joining items in _sequence_, separated by _separator_.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.array.first
documentation: Return the first item of collection _obj_.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.array.last
documentation: Return the last item of array-like object _obj_.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.array.concat
documentation: Return the concatenation of _sequence_ and _second_array_.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.array.map_
documentation: Return an array/list of items in _sequence_ selected by _key_.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.array.reverse
documentation: Reverses the order of the items in an array.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.array.sort
documentation:
Return a copy of _sequence_ in ascending order.

When a key string is provided, objects without the key property will
be at the end of the output list/array.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.array.sort_natural
documentation:
Return a copy of _sequence_ in ascending order, with case-insensitive comparison.

When a key string is provided, objects without the key property will
be at the end of the output list/array.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.array.where
documentation: Return a list of items from _sequence_ where _attr_ equals _value_.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.array.uniq
documentation: Return a copy of _sequence_ with duplicate elements removed.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.array.compact
documentation: Return a copy of _sequence_ with any NULL values removed.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.array.sum_
documentation:
Return the sum of all numeric elements in _sequence_.

If _key_ is given, it is assumed that sequence items are mapping-like,
and the values at _item[key]_ will be summed instead.

classes.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.array._Null
documentation: A null without a token for use in the map filter.

modules.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.math
documentation: Maths related filter functions.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.math.abs_
documentation: Return the absolute value of number _num_.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.math.at_most
documentation: Return _val_ or _other_, whichever is smaller.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.math.at_least
documentation: Return _val_ or _other_, whichever is greater.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.math.ceil
documentation: Return _num_ rounded up to the next integer.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.math.divided_by
documentation:
Return the result of dividing _num_ by _other_.

If both _num_ and _other_ are integers, integer division is performed.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.math.floor
documentation: Return _num_ rounded down to the next integer.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.math.minus
documentation: Return the result of subtracting _other_ from _num_.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.math.plus
documentation: Return the result of adding _other_ to _num_.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.math.round_
documentation: Returns the result of rounding _num_ to _digits_ decimal digits.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.math.times
documentation: Return the result of multiplying _num_ by _other_.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.filters.math.modulo
documentation: Return the remainder of dividing _num_ by _other_.

documentation: Scan Liquid template _source_ and return a list of Markup objects.

classes.name: liquid2.lexer.Lexer

methods.name: liquid2.lexer.Lexer.run
documentation: Populate _self.tokens_.

methods.name: liquid2.lexer.Lexer.next
documentation: Return the next character, or the empty string if no more characters.

methods.name: liquid2.lexer.Lexer.ignore
documentation: Ignore characters up to the pointer.

methods.name: liquid2.lexer.Lexer.backup
documentation: Move the pointer back one character.

methods.name: liquid2.lexer.Lexer.peek
documentation: Return the next character without advancing the pointer.

methods.name: liquid2.lexer.Lexer.accept
documentation: Match _pattern_ starting from the current position.

methods.name: liquid2.lexer.Lexer.ignore_whitespace
documentation: Move the pointer past any whitespace.

methods.name: liquid2.lexer.Lexer.consume_whitespace
documentation: Consume and return whitespace.

methods.name: liquid2.lexer.Lexer.ignore_line_space
documentation: Move the pointer past any allowed whitespace inside line statements.

methods.name: liquid2.lexer.Lexer.error
documentation: Emit an error token.

class_variables.name: liquid2.lexer.Lexer.skip
documentation: Alias for `ignore()`.
Liquid template lexical scanner.

classes.name: liquid2.builtin.loaders.dict_loader.CachingDictLoader
documentation: A `DictLoader` that caches parsed templates in memory.

modules.name: liquid2.builtin.loaders.choice_loader
documentation: A template loader that delegates to other template loaders.

classes.name: liquid2.builtin.loaders.choice_loader.ChoiceLoader

methods.name: liquid2.builtin.loaders.choice_loader.ChoiceLoader.get_source
documentation: Get source information for a template.

methods.name: liquid2.builtin.loaders.choice_loader.ChoiceLoader.get_source_async
documentation: Get source information for a template.

A template loader that delegates to other template loaders.

Args:
    loaders: A list of loaders implementing `liquid.loaders.BaseLoader`.

classes.name: liquid2.builtin.loaders.choice_loader.CachingChoiceLoader
documentation:
A `ChoiceLoader` that caches parsed templates in memory.

Args:
    loaders: A list of loaders implementing `liquid.loaders.BaseLoader`.
    auto_reload: If `True`, automatically reload a cached template if it has been
        updated.
    namespace_key: The name of a global render context variable or loader keyword
        argument that resolves to the current loader "namespace" or "scope".
    capacity: The maximum number of templates to hold in the cache before removing
        the least recently used template.

modules.name: liquid2.builtin.loaders.caching_file_system_loader
documentation: A file system loader that caches parsed templates in memory.

classes.name: liquid2.builtin.loaders.caching_file_system_loader.CachingFileSystemLoader
documentation:
A file system loader that caches parsed templates in memory.

Args:
    search_path: One or more paths to search.
    encoding: Open template files with the given encoding.
    ext: A default file extension. Should include a leading period.
    auto_reload: If `True`, automatically reload a cached template if it has been
        updated.
    namespace_key: The name of a global render context variable or loader keyword
        argument that resolves to the current loader "namespace" or "scope".

        If you're developing a multi-user application, a good namespace might be
        `uid`, where `uid` is a unique identifier for a user and templates are
        arranged in folders named for each `uid` inside the search path.
    capacity: The maximum number of templates to hold in the cache before removing
        the least recently used template.

modules.name: liquid2.builtin.loaders.file_system_loader
documentation: Built-in file system loader.

classes.name: liquid2.builtin.loaders.file_system_loader.FileSystemLoader

methods.name: liquid2.builtin.loaders.file_system_loader.FileSystemLoader.resolve_path
documentation:
Return a path to the template identified by _template_name_.

If the search path is a list of paths, returns the first path where
_template_name_ exists. If none of the search paths contain _template_name_, a
_TemplateNotFound_ exception is raised.

methods.name: liquid2.builtin.loaders.file_system_loader.FileSystemLoader.get_source
documentation: Get source information for a template.

methods.name: liquid2.builtin.loaders.file_system_loader.FileSystemLoader.get_source_async
documentation: Get source information for a template.

A loader that loads templates from one or more directories on the file system.

Args:
    search_path: One or more paths to search.
    encoding: Encoding to use when opening files.
    ext: A default file extension. Should include a leading period.

modules.name: liquid2.builtin.tags.extends_tag
documentation: The standard _extends_ and _block_ tags.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.tags.extends_tag._build_block_stacks
documentation:
Build a stack for each `{% block %}` in the inheritance chain.

Blocks defined in the base template will be at the top of the stack.

Args:
    context: A render context to build the block stacks in.
    template: A leaf template with an `extends` tag.
    parent_name: The name of the immediate parent template as a string literal.
    tag: The name of the `extends` tag, if it is overridden.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.tags.extends_tag._build_block_stacks_async
documentation:
Build a stack for each `{% block %}` in the inheritance chain.

Blocks defined in the base template will be at the top of the stack.

Args:
    context: A render context to build the block stacks in.
    template: A leaf template with an `extends` tag.
    parent_name: The name of the immediate parent template as a string.
    tag: The name of the `extends` tag, if it is overridden.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.tags.extends_tag._find_inheritance_nodes
documentation: Return lists of `extends` and `block` nodes from the given template.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.tags.extends_tag._stack_blocks
documentation:
Find template inheritance nodes in `template`.

Each node found is pushed on to the appropriate block stack.

modules.name: liquid2.environment
documentation: Template parsing and rendering configuration.

classes.name: liquid2.environment.Environment

methods.name: liquid2.environment.Environment.setup_tags_and_filters
documentation:
Add tags and filters to this environment.

This is called once when initializing an environment. Override this method
in your custom environments.

methods.name: liquid2.environment.Environment.parse
documentation: Compile template source text and return an abstract syntax tree.

methods.name: liquid2.environment.Environment.from_string
documentation: Create a template from a string.

methods.name: liquid2.environment.Environment.get_template
documentation:
Load and parse a template using the configured loader.

Args:
    name: The template's name. The loader is responsible for interpreting
        the name. It could be the name of a file or some other identifier.
    globals: A mapping of render context variables attached to the
        resulting template.
    context: An optional render context that can be used to narrow the template
        source search space.
    kwargs: Arbitrary arguments that can be used to narrow the template source
        search space.

Raises:
    TemplateNotFound: If a template with the given name can not be found.

methods.name: liquid2.environment.Environment.get_template_async
documentation: An async version of `get_template()`.

methods.name: liquid2.environment.Environment.make_globals
documentation: Combine environment globals with template globals.

methods.name: liquid2.environment.Environment.trim
documentation: Return _text_ after applying whitespace control.

class_variables.name: liquid2.environment.Environment.context_depth_limit
documentation:
Maximum number of times a render context can be extended or wrapped before
raising a `ContextDepthError`.

class_variables.name: liquid2.environment.Environment.loop_iteration_limit
documentation:
Maximum number of loop iterations allowed before a `LoopIterationLimitError` is
raised.

class_variables.name: liquid2.environment.Environment.local_namespace_limit
documentation:
Maximum number of bytes (according to sys.getsizeof) allowed in a template's
local namespace before a `LocalNamespaceLimitError` is raised. We only count the
size of the namespaces values, not the size of keys/names.

class_variables.name: liquid2.environment.Environment.output_stream_limit
documentation:
Maximum number of bytes that can be written to a template's output stream before
raising an `OutputStreamLimitError`.

class_variables.name: liquid2.environment.Environment.suppress_blank_control_flow_blocks
documentation:
If True (the default), indicates that blocks rendering to whitespace only will
not be output.
Template parsing and rendering configuration.

modules.name: liquid2.utils.text
documentation: Text processing utilities.

functions.name: liquid2.utils.text.truncate_chars
documentation:
Truncates a string if it is longer than the specified number of characters.
Truncated strings will end with `end`, an ellipsis by default.

functions.name: liquid2.utils.text.truncate_words
documentation: Truncates a string down to the number of words passed as the first parameter.
Note: truncate_words is no longer used by the truncatewords filter.

modules.name: liquid2.utils.chainmap
documentation:
A read-only chain map.

Based on "A greatly simplified read-only version of Chainmap" by Raymond Hettinger.
https://code.activestate.com/recipes/305268/

classes.name: liquid2.utils.chainmap.ReadOnlyChainMap

methods.name: liquid2.utils.chainmap.ReadOnlyChainMap.size

methods.name: liquid2.utils.chainmap.ReadOnlyChainMap.get
documentation: Return the value for key if key is in the dictionary, else default.

methods.name: liquid2.utils.chainmap.ReadOnlyChainMap.push
documentation: Add a mapping to the front of the chain map.

methods.name: liquid2.utils.chainmap.ReadOnlyChainMap.pop
documentation: Remove a mapping from the front of the chain map.
Combine multiple mappings for sequential lookup.

modules.name: liquid2.static_analysis
documentation: Analyze variable, tag and filter usage by traversing a template's syntax tree.

classes.name: liquid2.static_analysis.Span
documentation: The location of a variable, tag or filter in a template.

classes.name: liquid2.static_analysis.Variable
documentation: A variable as sequence of segments that make up its path and its location.

methods.name: liquid2.static_analysis._StaticScope.add
documentation: Add a name to the root/template scope.

classes.name: liquid2.static_analysis.TemplateAnalysis
documentation:
The result of analyzing a template using `Template.analyze()`.

Args:
    variables: All referenced variables, whether they are in scope or not.
        Including references to names such as `forloop` from the `for` tag.
    locals: Template variables that are added to the template local
        scope, whether they are subsequently used or not.
    globals: Template variables that, on the given line number and
        "file", are out of scope or are assumed to be "global". That is, expected to
        be included by the application developer rather than a template author.
    filters: All filters found during static analysis.
    tags: All tags found during static analysis.

modules.name: liquid2.builtin.expressions
documentation: Expression for built in, standard tags.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.expressions.is_empty
documentation: Return True if _obj_ is considered empty.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.expressions.is_blank
documentation: Return True if _obj_ is considered blank.

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.expressions.parse_primitive

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.expressions.parse_string_or_path

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.expressions.parse_keyword_arguments
documentation:
Parse _tokens_ into a list or keyword arguments.

Argument keys and values can be separated by a colon (`:`) or an equals sign
(`=`).

functions.name: liquid2.builtin.expressions.is_truthy
documentation: Return _True_ if _obj_ is considered Liquid truthy.

methods.name: liquid2.builtin.expressions.Path.segments
documentation:
Return this path's segments as a tuple of strings and ints.

Segments can also be nested tuples of strings, ints and tuples if the path
contains nested paths.
